data.title: Wire .archived marker write into ticket-lifecycle.sh after each ARCHIVED event write
data.description:
## Testing Mode
UPDATE

## What
Modify ticket-lifecycle.sh to invoke write_marker() from ticket_reducer.marker after each ARCHIVED event file is written to disk AND after the final git commit. The marker write must be error-tolerant (a failed marker write must NOT prevent the event commit from proceeding).

## Implementation

In ticket-lifecycle.sh, in the ARCHIVED event writing loop (around line 132-138 where event_file is written):

After the event file is written to disk (before git add/commit), add the marker write:

```bash
# Write .archived marker (error-tolerant — degraded to slow path on failure)
python3 -c "
import sys
sys.path.insert(0, '$SCRIPT_DIR')
from ticket_reducer.marker import write_marker
write_marker('$ticket_dir')
" 2>/dev/null || true
```

Place this AFTER the event file is flushed to disk AND BEFORE (or as part of) the git commit, so the marker and event are in the same commit.

## Write ordering constraint (SC 2)
The marker write MUST happen after the event JSON file is flushed to disk. The marker goes into the same git commit as the ARCHIVED event — both are staged together before the single `git commit` at the end of the lifecycle script.

## Error tolerance
The `|| true` ensures marker write failure does not abort the lifecycle script. Use stderr redirect (`2>/dev/null`) to suppress the marker module's own stderr on failure (the test environment may not have the module on path; production always will).

## Files to modify
- plugins/dso/scripts/ticket-lifecycle.sh (MODIFY — add marker write call in the archival loop)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] test_lifecycle_writes_archived_marker from T5 now passes
  Verify: bash $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/tests/scripts/test-ticket-lifecycle.sh test_lifecycle_writes_archived_marker
- [ ] All existing lifecycle tests still pass
  Verify: bash $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/tests/scripts/test-ticket-lifecycle.sh
- [ ] Marker write is error-tolerant: lifecycle script exits 0 even if python3/marker module is unavailable
  Verify: bash -c 'PATH=/nonexistent $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/plugins/dso/scripts/ticket-lifecycle.sh --help 2>/dev/null || true; test $? -le 1'
- [ ] bash tests/run-all.sh exits 0
  Verify: cd $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel) && bash tests/run-all.sh
